text.title: error-prevention Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for error-prevention as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: User wastes effort filling subsequent fields after making an error in an early field. Inline validation catches errors while context is fresh and the field is still in focus.

failure-mode.description: Adding confirmation dialogs to every action.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dialog fatigue causes users to dismiss confirmations without reading them, defeating protection for genuinely dangerous actions. Reserve confirmations for irreversible, high-consequence actions only.

failure-mode.description: Agent reviews only the frontend form without also reviewing the API handler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Client-side validation alone is insufficient. A missing constraint in the API handler allows invalid data from any client (API, curl, other frontends). Always audit both layers in the same pass.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates Zod/Pydantic schemas that miss cross-field validation rules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schemas generated from field lists do not account for dependencies (end date must be after start date, password must match confirmation). Flag cross-field validations as needing manual implementation.
